name: Living Resume
summary: A recruiter-facing digital twin that answers grounded resume questions and performs candid fit analysis.
problem: Traditional portfolios are passive and do not demonstrate how someone thinks or how they execute in AI-native environments.
actions: Added structured AI explainers behind each role to make resume claims inspectable.
Chose a low-cost deployment strategy that still preserves architectural portability.
outcomes: Transforms resume review into an active conversation.
Demonstrates product, architecture, and AI-system thinking in one artifact.
Creates a reusable showcase for collaboration and hiring conversations.

name: Agentic Delivery Framework
summary: Defined a role-based agent workflow with explicit handoffs, artifacts, and review gates for shipping AI-native products.
problem: Agentic workflows often sound impressive but collapse under unclear ownership and poor artifact boundaries.
actions: Separated delivery into product, content, AI architecture, application, QA, and ops roles.
Required every handoff to include decisions, assumptions, and acceptance criteria.
Integrated process documentation into the final product instead of hiding it.
outcomes: Made the delivery model itself part of the portfolio story.
Created reusable templates for future projects.

name: Role Fit Analysis Workflow
summary: A structured evaluator that compares external job requirements against curated evidence from the resume corpus.
problem: Recruiters need a quick, trustworthy way to understand how experience maps to a role without reading everything manually.
actions: Defined a scoring rubric for domain, execution, AI fluency, and leadership.
Separated proven experience from adjacent experience and unsupported claims.
Designed support for paste, upload, and URL job-description intake.
outcomes: Makes fit evaluation faster and more transparent.
Shows candor instead of generic self-promotion.
Adds a concrete AI-native workflow beyond basic chat.
